tiers.core: Fast, cheap, good for simple tasks. Free tier models.
tiers.ultra: Best quality, highest cost. Use sparingly.

models.best_use_case: Most complex tasks, agentic coding, deep reasoning, multi-step orchestration
models.notes: Most intelligent model. 1M context at standard pricing. Fast mode available at 6x premium ($30/$150). US-only inference at 1.1x premium.

models.best_use_case: Production workloads balancing intelligence, speed, and cost. General-purpose default.
models.notes: 1M context at standard pricing. Best cost-to-intelligence ratio. US-only inference at 1.1x premium.

models.best_use_case: High-throughput, low-latency tasks. Classification, data extraction, real-time chat.
models.notes: Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence. No adaptive thinking.

models.best_use_case: Previous-gen balanced model. Upgrade to Sonnet 4.6 when ready.
models.notes: 1M context available via context-1m-2025-08-07 beta header (premium pricing above 200k: $6/$22.50). Retires not sooner than Sep 29, 2026.

models.best_use_case: High-intelligence tasks. Upgrade to Opus 4.6 when ready.
models.notes: Retires not sooner than Nov 24, 2026.

models.best_use_case: Legacy high-intelligence model. Migrate to Opus 4.6 for 3x cost reduction.
models.notes: Retires not sooner than Aug 5, 2026. Significantly more expensive than Opus 4.5/4.6.

models.best_use_case: Legacy balanced model. Upgrade to Sonnet 4.6.
models.notes: 1M context available via beta header. Retires not sooner than May 14, 2026.

models.best_use_case: Legacy high-intelligence model. Migrate to Opus 4.6 for 3x cost reduction.
models.notes: Retires not sooner than May 14, 2026. Same pricing as Opus 4.1.

models.best_use_case: Deprecated. Migrate to Sonnet 4.6.
models.notes: Deprecated model. Still functional but not recommended.

models.best_use_case: Budget-friendly high-throughput tasks. Data labeling, content moderation, simple extraction.
models.notes: Cheapest non-Haiku-3 model still active. Consider migrating to Haiku 4.5 for 20% more cost but significantly better performance and extended thinking.

models.best_use_case: Deprecated. Cheapest model available but being retired. Migrate to Haiku 4.5.
models.notes: Deprecated. Retires April 19, 2026. No API alias — must use full model ID.

models.best_use_case: Ultra-fast, ultra-cheap inference; simple tasks, classification, high-throughput pipelines
High-quality reasoning and general-purpose tasks at blazing speed
Large MoE model for complex reasoning; free tier available with rate limits
Highest-intelligence model on Cerebras; complex reasoning, multilingual
Multimodal MoE model with vision and function calling; strong general-purpose
OpenAI open-weight model for high-reasoning tasks; production-grade
Frontier MoE model with 256K context, vision, and tool calling
High-quality 70B model for complex tasks; FP8 quantized for efficiency
Same pricing as 3.3; consider 3.3 for newer training data
Cheapest 8B model for high-throughput simple tasks
Smallest model; edge deployment, classification, ultra-low cost
Small model for simple text tasks at low cost
Lightweight vision model for image understanding tasks
Reasoning-optimized distilled model for complex logic and math
Balanced mid-range model with function calling
Dedicated coding model for code generation and analysis
Google's efficient open model; good for general-purpose chat
General-purpose chat and coding; extremely cost-effective at $0.27/M input. Great for high-volume production use.
Complex reasoning, math, and logic problems. Chain-of-thought reasoning with 32K CoT budget.

models.bestUseCase: Complex reasoning, deep code analysis, architectural reviews, long-context research tasks. Use when quality matters more than cost.
models.notes: Default maxOutputTokens is 8192; must explicitly set to 65536. Context-window-tiered pricing kicks in at 200K tokens. Google's most capable general reasoning model in the 2.x line.

models.bestUseCase: High-volume processing with good reasoning quality. Sweet spot between cost and capability. Great for agentic coding, document analysis, and batch workloads.
models.notes: Best value-for-quality ratio in the 2.x lineup. 85% cheaper than Pro for most tasks. Default maxOutputTokens is 8192; must explicitly set higher.

models.bestUseCase: Ultra-low-cost tasks: classification, tagging, extraction, simple Q&A, high-throughput batch processing. Optimized for speed and cost over reasoning depth.
models.notes: Cheapest Gemini 2.x model. Free tier available. Not recommended for complex reasoning tasks.

models.bestUseCase: Legacy workloads. Migrate to gemini-2.5-flash-lite or gemini-3-flash-preview.
models.notes: DEPRECATED — shutting down June 1, 2026. Use gemini-2.5-flash-lite for comparable pricing. Free tier available with 60 RPM / 1000 RPD.

models.bestUseCase: Cheapest possible Gemini model. Migrate to gemini-2.5-flash-lite.
models.notes: DEPRECATED — shutting down June 1, 2026. Absolute cheapest Gemini at $0.075/$0.30. No audio/video input support.

models.bestUseCase: Text embeddings for RAG, semantic search, and clustering. Older model being superseded by Gemini Embedding 2.
models.notes: 768-dimensional embeddings. Single modality (text only). Being replaced by Gemini Embedding 2 Preview.

models.bestUseCase: Multimodal semantic search, cross-modal RAG, unified embedding across text/image/audio/video.
models.notes: Google's first natively multimodal embedding model. Maps text, images, audio, video, and documents into a single embedding space. 3072-dimensional embeddings.

models.bestUseCase: Native image generation and editing within conversations. Inline image creation without external tools.
models.notes: Experimental native image generation. Same pricing as base 2.0 Flash. Output images consume additional tokens.

models.best_use_case: High-quality open-source model with tool use support; good balance of quality and speed
Multimodal MoE model — fast, cheap, handles images; great for vision+text tasks
Cheapest Groq option; instant responses for simple tasks, classification, and high-volume workloads
Strong multilingual model with reasoning; good mid-range option
OpenAI's open-weight model for high-reasoning tasks at low cost
Lightweight GPT-OSS for fast reasoning at near-free pricing
Massive 1T MoE model for complex agentic tasks and tool calling
Ultra-efficient open-source MoE model; strong on SWE-Bench and coding; best cost/performance ratio
Xiaomi's flagship model with 1M context; deeply optimized for agentic scenarios and complex reasoning
Omni-modal model natively processing image, video, and audio inputs in a unified architecture
Top-tier Mistral model for complex reasoning, long-context analysis, and enterprise workloads
Balanced quality/cost for general-purpose tasks; strong coding and reasoning
Ultra-cheap high-throughput model for classification, extraction, simple Q&A
Dedicated coding model — code generation, completion, and FIM with 256K context
Multimodal vision model for image understanding, document parsing, and visual Q&A
Edge/mobile deployment, ultra-low-cost classification, and on-device inference
Small but capable model for cost-sensitive production workloads with long context
Mid-range small model balancing cost and capability for production use
Versatile high-intelligence flagship for text+vision tasks, code generation, and general-purpose use. Best all-rounder for production apps.
models.notes: Flagship model. 128K context, 16.4K max output.

models.best_use_case: Budget-friendly high-volume processing, classification, extraction, quick chats, fine-tuning distillation from GPT-4o.
models.notes: Previous-gen budget option. 128K context, 16.4K max output.

models.best_use_case: Long-context champion—process entire codebases, legal docs, book-length content. Strong instruction following and tool calling.
models.notes: 1M token context window. 32.8K max output.

models.best_use_case: Budget long-context processing—summarize large documents, extract data from long inputs, budget-friendly RAG with large context.
models.notes: 1M token context window. 32.8K max output. Smaller, faster GPT-4.1.

models.best_use_case: Ultra-low-cost classification, routing, entity extraction, sentiment analysis, data labeling at massive scale.
models.notes: 1M token context window. 32.8K max output. Cheapest GPT-4.1-class model.

models.best_use_case: Research-grade deep reasoning, complex math, scientific analysis, multi-step problem solving. Original flagship reasoning model.
models.notes: Previous full o-series reasoning model. Chain-of-thought reasoning. Expensive—o3 is 7.5x cheaper.

models.best_use_case: Budget reasoning for math and coding puzzles. Superseded by o3-mini which has higher intelligence at same price.
models.notes: Deprecated—use o3-mini instead. Text-only, no vision, no function calling, no structured outputs.

models.best_use_case: Advanced reasoning for math, science, coding, visual reasoning, and technical writing. Think through multi-step problems.
models.notes: Reasoning model. Succeeded by GPT-5. 200K context, 100K max output.

models.best_use_case: Budget reasoning—moderate math, code review, logic puzzles where cost matters. Same price as o1-mini but higher intelligence.
models.notes: Small reasoning model. Text-only. Supports function calling and structured outputs (unlike o1-mini).

models.best_use_case: Fast, cost-efficient reasoning for coding and visual tasks. Optimized for speed with vision support (unlike o3-mini).
models.notes: Latest small o-series model. Succeeded by GPT-5 mini. Supports fine-tuning.

models.best_use_case: Fine-tuned o4-mini for Codex CLI—agentic coding workflows, code generation, code review in CLI environments.
models.notes: Fine-tuned o4-mini for Codex CLI. For direct API use, OpenAI recommends gpt-4.1.

models.best_use_case: Legacy high-intelligence model. Superseded by GPT-4o (cheaper, better). Use only for existing integrations.
models.notes: Legacy model. 128K context but only 4K max output. No structured outputs, no fine-tuning. Very expensive—migrate away.

models.best_use_case: Complex reasoning, analysis, and high-quality generation tasks where cost is secondary to quality
Cost-effective reasoning tasks, quick Q&A, and high-throughput applications
Fast, cheap inference with massive 2M context; great for long-doc analysis and high-volume workloads
Latest flagship model — lowest hallucination rate, strict prompt adherence, complex agentic workflows
Optimized for code generation and coding tasks at low cost
